title:
Lesson 1:
At the doctor
description: Medical vocabulary: enfermo/enferma (sick), dolor (pain), medicina (medicine), doctor/doctora, hospital.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: I don't feel well.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: What hurts?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: My stomach hurts.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: You should go to the doctor.

title:
Lesson 2:
Feeling words
description: Emotions with estar: cansado (tired), feliz (happy), triste (sad), enojado (angry), nervioso (nervous).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: How do you feel?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: I am very happy today.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: I am a little tired.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Why are you tired?

title:
Lesson 3:
Describing things
description: Adjectives for things: grande (big), pequeño (small), nuevo (new), viejo (old), bonito (pretty), feo (ugly).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Your house is very big.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Yes, it's big and new.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: My apartment is small.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: But it's pretty.

title:
Lesson 4:
Expressing necessity
description: 'Tener que' + infinitive = to have to do something. 'Necesitar' = to need. Expressing obligations.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: What do you have to do today?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: I have to work.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: I have to study.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: I need to finish a project.

title:
Lesson 5:
Expressing doubt
description: Expressing uncertainty: 'No estoy seguro' (I'm not sure), 'Tal vez' (Maybe), 'Quizás' (Perhaps), 'Probablemente' (Probably).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Is it going to rain tomorrow?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: I'm not sure. Maybe.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Are you going to the party?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Probably yes.

title:
Lesson 6:
Review and celebration
description: Congratulations on reaching lesson 100! Review key concepts: greetings, verbs, time, daily life, past tense. Keep practicing!

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: We did it! We finished one hundred lessons.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Yes, now I speak more Spanish.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: We learned a lot.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: It's true. I am very happy.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Congratulations! Keep practicing!

title:
Lesson 7:
I Love It / I Like It
description: 'Me encanta' = I love it (stronger than me gusta). 'Me gusta' = I like it. 'Me gusta mucho' = I like it a lot. These use indirect object pronouns — the subject is the thing, not you.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Did you try the new restaurant?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Sí, me encanta. La comida es increíble.
dialogue.english: Yes, I love it. The food is incredible.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Did you like the dessert too?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Me gustó mucho. Voy a volver esta semana.
dialogue.english: I liked it a lot. I'm going back this week.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: ¡Yo también quiero ir! Me gusta probar lugares nuevos.
dialogue.english: I want to go too! I like trying new places.

title:
Lesson 8:
It Bothers Me / My Mood
description: 'Me molesta' = It bothers me. 'Me molesta mucho' = It really bothers me. 'Estoy de buen humor' = I'm in a good mood. 'Estoy de mal humor' = I'm in a bad mood.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Are you okay? You seem bothered.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Estoy de mal humor. Me molesta mucho el ruido.
dialogue.english: I'm in a bad mood. The noise really bothers me.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: I understand. Can I do anything?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: No, tranquila. Mañana estaré de buen humor.
dialogue.english: No, it's okay. Tomorrow I'll be in a good mood.

title:
Lesson 9:
Fun / Boring
description: 'Está divertido' = It's fun / It's entertaining. 'Está aburrido' = It's boring. Both use 'estar' because they describe temporary states. Note: 'Estoy aburrido/a' = I'm bored (about yourself).

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: How was the party?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Estuvo muy divertida. Bailamos toda la noche.
dialogue.english: It was really fun. We danced all night.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: And the movie before?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Esa estuvo aburrida. Me quedé dormido.
dialogue.english: That one was boring. I fell asleep.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: ¡Ja! A mí también me pareció aburrida.
dialogue.english: Ha! I also found it boring.

title:
Lesson 10:
I'm Distracted / I Need to Focus
description: 'Estoy distraído/distraída' = I'm distracted (use -o if male, -a if female). 'Necesito enfocarme' = I need to focus. 'Necesito poner atención' = I need to pay attention. 'Estaba pensando en otra cosa' = I was thinking about something else.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: ¿Me escuchaste? Dije tu nombre tres veces.
dialogue.english: Did you hear me? I said your name three times.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Perdón, estaba pensando en otra cosa.
dialogue.english: Sorry, I was thinking about something else.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: You're always distracted lately.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: You're right. I need to focus more.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: Sobre todo en el trabajo. Necesitas poner atención.
dialogue.english: Especially at work. You need to pay attention.

vocabulary.english: I was thinking about something else
